resources.string: Five steps and the Tesla bridge is ready.
So the app can store messages in a way your Tesla reads.
Reads notifications from your messenger apps.
For this app\'s status and error messages.

So your Tesla shows the name instead of a placeholder number, the app creates an invisible entry per conversation. It does not appear in your Contacts app. Without this permission Tesla shows the number.
Skip — Tesla will then only show the number
Permission granted. Existing conversations are backfilled in the background.
Checks whether the test address (%1$s) is rejected free of charge by your network. Sends a 1-character SMS, result after ~60 s.

The test sends a 1-character SMS over your mobile network to the reserved test address %1$s. If your provider rejects it correctly, no costs are incurred — but depending on the provider an SMS may be charged.\n\nContinue?
Network let the test SMS through
Tesla replies to %1$s could be sent as a regular SMS and incur costs. If you want to continue, confirm that you accept the risk.
→ A different package is set as default. Tap \'Set\' again and explicitly choose \'Tesla Messages Manager\' in the dialog.
